papers.title_en: Emerging Flexible Designs for Geospatial Multimodal Foundation Models
papers.authors: Philipe Dias, Waqwoya Abebe, Abhishek Potnis, Aristeidis Tsaris, Dan Lu, Xiao Wang, Dalton Lunga
papers.evidence: 地理空间多模态基础模型用于遥感分类
papers.tldr: 地理空间基础模型架构多样，难以公平比较性能。本研究在统一自监督学习目标和数据集下，对多种主流架构进行标准化的公平对比，并在GEOBench基准上评估分类与分割任务。结果揭示了模型灵活性、模态对齐与下游性能之间的设计权衡。这些见解为构建下一代鲁棒多模态地理空间模型提供了实用指导。
papers.abstract_en: Foundation models are rapidly transforming Earth observation by enabling scalable pretraining across diverse unlabeled geospatial modalities. However, their architectural diversity ranging from encoder-only to encoder-decoder and masked autoencoding paradigms makes it challenging to assess performance trade offs in a consistent manner. In this work, we present an apples-to-apples comparison of leading FM architectures designed for geospatial multimodal reasoning, with a particular focus on flexibility across varied spectral band configurations. We standardize pretraining using identical self supervised learning objectives and training datasets, and evaluate all models under consistent parameterization on the GEOBench benchmark across classification and segmentation tasks. Our results offer new insights into the design trade-offs between model flexibility, modality alignment, and downstream task performance. By highlighting architectural strengths and limitations under controlled conditions, this study provides practical guidance for building next generation geospatial foundation models capable of robust multimodal reasoning.

papers.title_en: FreqKD: Frequency-Decoupled Cross-Modal Knowledge Distillation for Infrared Object Detection
papers.authors: Keval Thaker, Venkatraman Narayanan, Abdalmalek Aburaddaha, Samir A. Rawashdeh
papers.evidence: 从RGB到红外的跨模态知识蒸馏用于目标检测
papers.tldr: 从RGB大模型向红外图像迁移知识面临模态差异挑战，现有蒸馏方法忽视频率特性。研究发现低频特征（形状、布局）跨模态对齐良好，而高频特征（纹理、边缘）差异显著。为此提出FreqKD，对低频采用严格MSE损失以保留共享结构，对高频采用松弛log-MSE损失以容忍纹理差异。在KAIST行人检测上提升2.4 mAP50，并有效迁移至FLIR和MFNet等任务，证明频域解耦蒸馏的有效性。
papers.abstract_en: Transfer learning from large-scale RGB foundation models to infrared (IR) imagery through knowledge distillation (KD) remains challenging due to fundamental differences in image formation physics. We investigate the spectral structure of the RGB--IR modality gap and observe that feature divergence is not uniform across spatial frequencies: low-frequency components (shape, layout) show greater cross-modal alignment than high-frequency components (texture, fine edges), which reflect modality-specific characteristics. Based on this analysis, we propose FreqKD, a frequency-decoupled distillation framework that applies asymmetric supervision adapted to each band's cross-modal consistency. The method employs strict mean squared error (MSE) on the low-frequency band to preserve shared structural information and a relaxed log-MSE loss (weighted at 0.1) on the high-frequency band to provide edge guidance while tolerating texture differences. Spectral divergence analysis on 500 paired samples shows that high-frequency divergence exceeds low-frequency divergence by a factor of 2.4x on average across all analysed transformer layers. On KAIST multispectral pedestrian detection, FreqKD achieves 64.1 mAP50, improving 2.4 points over the DINOv2 baseline. The learned representation transfers across datasets (FLIR ADAS, +2.1 mAP50), tasks (MFNet segmentation, +1.85 mean intersection-over-union), and architectures (ResNet-50, +1.0 mAP50). Code is available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/freq_decoupled_kd-5E5A
